title: Safety Behavior Identification Checklist
description: Identify the safety behaviors that may be maintaining your panic cycle. Check off the behaviors you currently use, then work on reducing them one at a time.
instructions: Go through each item honestly and check off the behaviors you use to cope with panic or anxiety. The checked items become your targets for gradual reduction. Revisit this checklist weekly to track your progress in dropping safety behaviors.

items.label: I carry medications 'just in case' (even if I rarely take them)
I always carry water or a beverage
I keep my phone in hand to call for help if needed
I carry other 'safety' items (paper bag, snack, etc.)
I sit or lie down immediately when I feel anxious
I lean against something stable (wall, counter, furniture)
I hold onto someone or something for support
I distract myself obsessively (phone, music, counting, games)
I repeatedly reassure myself with phrases or mental rituals
I check my pulse, breathing, or body symptoms obsessively
I leave the situation or make excuses to escape
I always sit near exits or in aisle seats
I avoid caffeine completely out of fear
I avoid exercise or physical exertion
I avoid hot places, hot showers, or heated environments
I avoid crowded places (shops, concerts, events)
I avoid being alone or going places without a trusted person
I avoid being far from home or from a hospital
I avoid public transportation, driving on highways, or bridges
I avoid elevators, tunnels, or enclosed spaces

disclaimer: This checklist is for educational self-awareness purposes. Identifying safety behaviors is an important step in recovery from panic disorder. If you need support reducing these behaviors, consider working with a CBT therapist. If you are in crisis, call or text 988.
